parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now answer the questions on your screen. So, let me take some details from you so that we can buy your train ticket. First of all, what's your full name?

Speaker 2: Angela Bradbury.

Speaker 1: Could you spell Bradbury for me?

Speaker 2: B-R-A-D-B-U-R-Y.

Speaker 1: And your address?

Speaker 2: 23 Brown Street, Toronto.

Speaker 1: And the postal code?

Speaker 2: M4C3DD. Oh, sorry, I mean TT. That's M4C3TT.

Speaker 1: Could I take a contact telephone number, please? Home or cell?

Speaker 2: I think cell is usually better. The cell is 07523 937616.

Speaker 1: Fine. And on what date and at what time do you wish to travel?

Speaker 2: I'll be in London from the 11th of April for five nights and then I can go to Paris.

Speaker 1: Will that be the 16th or the 17th?

Speaker 2: The 16th. That's right. And I'd like to go in the early afternoon so I won't be rushed. Around 2 o'clock would be good.

Speaker 1: There's a train at 2.20 that afternoon.

Speaker 2: Oh, that will be perfect. I guess you don't want a return ticket as you're already booked to Canada from Paris to Toronto.

Speaker 1: That's right. What is the arrival time of that train?

Speaker 2: Let's see. It departs at 2.20 and arrives in Paris at 5.20.

Speaker 1: Okay, that's fine. I didn't ask this before, but of course you can travel first or economy class. What would you like?

Speaker 2: What's the difference?

Speaker 1: The economy is £50 and for that you get a reserved seat from London to Paris. If you book first class, then you get a bigger seat, lunch with drinks is served at your seat and everything looks a bit nicer. That comes in at £108. It's quite a bit more, but you do get the special treatment.

Speaker 2: I'm not that worried about lunch. If we leave at 2.20, then I would have eaten already and I want to be hungry for a great first French meal in Paris, so I'll go economy.

Speaker 1: So, the exact cost in Canadian dollars is $81.35. How would you like to pay?

Speaker 2: With this Mastercard, please.

Speaker 1: Could you read me the number, please?

Speaker 2: 8362-6028-6481-4724.

Speaker 1: Thank you very much. So, here is your reservation sheet and economy ticket confirmation. You will see that you are on train number EU621 and you have seat 30C. To remind you, it departs at 2.20 and arrives in Paris at 5.20. Can you read through and check the details for me, please?

Speaker 2: Let's see. That's all fine. Thank you very much.

Speaker 1: You're welcome, madam. Have a wonderful time in Paris.

Speaker 2: Thank you. Goodbye.

Speaker 1: Goodbye.
